channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Mapfre compra Safety por 1.542 millones de dólares para reforzar su negocio en EE.UU.
item.description: Mapfre ha alcanzado un acuerdo para adquirir la aseguradora estadounidense Safety Insurance Group por 1.542 millones de dólares (unos 1.300 millones de euros), una operación con la que reforzará su posición en el mercado del nordeste de Estados Unidos y que supondrá la creación de la segunda mayor aseguradora de automóvil privado de Nueva Inglaterra.

item.title: Nara Seguros y Denarius se alían para ofrecer seguros de salud al sector sanitario
item.description: Nara Seguros y Denarius han suscrito un acuerdo de colaboración por el que la plataforma incorporará a su oferta los seguros de salud de Nara Seguros, dirigidos a profesionales sanitarios, trabajadores autónomos, clínicas y empresas del sector.

item.title: Generali logra el respaldo del 92% de la representación sindical en su nuevo Convenio Colectivo
item.description: Generali ha firmado su nuevo Convenio Colectivo con la representación de los trabajadores, culminando el proceso de integración laboral de sus plantillas y estableciendo un marco común para toda la organización para el periodo 2026-2029.

item.title: Hybrids Boom as EVs Fall Out of Favor Even in California
item.description: Hybrids accounted for almost one in every four automobiles registered in California in the second quarter, according to newly released data from the California New Car Dealers Association. Hybrids, meanwhile, have benefited as the war in Iran has pushed gas prices higher. The national average was $4.02 for a gallon of gas as of Tuesday, while California’s is $5.52, according to AAA.

item.title: NJ High Court Rules Allstate’s $1.7M RICO Claims Fraud Suit Merits Trial, Not Arbitration
item.description: New Jersey’s high court has ruled that Allstate Insurance has a right to a jury trial for its fraud and conspiracy claims brought against more than 30 medical providers under the state’s Insurance Fraud Prevention Act (IFPA) and the Anti-Racketeering Act (RICO). In 2023, the trial court had dismissed the claims and ordered arbitration, reasoning that AICRA’s language requires arbitration of all disputes around the recovery of PIP benefits. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit that found that similar claims in a $10 million fraud lawsuit brought by GEICO against three chiropractors were subject to arbitration In New Jersey, per state law and arbitration agreements.

item.title: Heat Worries US Wheat Farmers Just as Crop Prices Are Surging
item.description: For farmers in North Dakota surveying their wheat as harvest closes in, scorching heat is threatening yields just as crop prices rise to a three-year high . Read More: Crop Prices Hit Three-Year High as Heat, War Stoke Supply Fears “It escalates and it de-escalates,” said Pellman, who also grows barley, canola, soybeans and corn. Speaking during the Wheat Quality Council’s annual spring and durum wheat crop tour, he was more focused on his own farm than the ongoing turmoil in Eastern Europe.

item.title: Mapfre to Acquire Safety Insurance in $1.5B in Cash Deal
item.description: Safety Insurance Group Inc,, a property/casualty insurer in Massachusetts and New England, has agreed to be acquired by Spain’s Mapfre S.A. will merge with and into Safety, following which Safety will become a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mapfre U.S.A. Safety shareholders will receive $105 for each Safety common share in cash, which represents a substantial premium of 44% on Safety’s stock price as of July 23, 2026.

item.title: This Year’s SCS Loss Totals Will Fall Below Average: KCC Analysis
item.description: KCC’s SCS Bulletin, however, does highlight why insured losses this year are coming in below average compared to prior years. According to a bulletin published by KCC, the risk modeling firm projects that severe convective storm losses for all of 2026 will drop below annual averages based on loss activity accumulated so far for the first six months. KCC did not provide insured loss or economic loss figures for this year or prior years in its bulletin or when contacted by Carrier Management, instead noting that its modeled estimates for the total industry losses are confidential (available only to clients).

item.title: HDI Global hires Byron Ernst as power and energy underwriter in Sydney
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Berkley Insurance Asia names Shivi Bhutani as liability lines underwriter in Gift City
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Shinhan Financial eyes non-life acquisition, confirms Lotte Non-Life interest
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Multilateralism, Utopia, and the Financial Stability Board
item.description: Over five hundred years ago, Saint Thomas More published Utopia , a short book about a society that is governed by complete social and economic cooperation; what we might call perfect multilateralism. In the next few minutes, rather than lamenting the demise of multilateralism, I want to explain why multilateralism remains essential in financial regulation, what the FSB’s experience has been, and outline some practical steps we can all take to keep multilateralism working. Established by the G20 (also a multilateral organization) after the Great Financial Crisis, the FSB’s mandate is to promote global financial stability.

item.title: Global property insurance rates fall 12% in Q2 2026, as soft market broadens: Marsh Risk
item.description: Broker Marsh Risk has reported that property rates declined 12% on a global basis in the second quarter of 2026, led by a sharp 19% decline in India, Middle East and Asia (IMEA), while double-digit decreases were also recorded in the United States, the United Kingdom, the Pacific, and Latin America and Caribbean. In its latest report on global commercial insurance rates, Marsh Risk highlighted how Q2 2026 marked the eighth consecutive quarter of rate decreases, which according to the firm, continues to be fueled by abundant capacity and strong insurer competition across all major product lines. This is likely to create additional opportunities for clients to improve coverage and refine program design, that may better position them for future market changes.” As previously mentioned, property rates declined by 12% in Q2 2026 on a global basis, following 9% decreases in Q1 2026 and Q4 2025, amid available capacity following favourable reinsurance conditions.

item.title: Mapfre to acquire Safety Insurance Group for $1.54bn to strengthen Northeast position
item.description: Corporation (Mapfre USA), has agreed to acquire Safety Insurance Group, Inc. This acquisition aligns with global re/insurer Mapfre’s long-term growth strategy, and aims to strengthen Mapfre USA’s position in Massachusetts and across the Northeastern region. Mapfre, an international insurance company, through its subsidiary, Mapfre U.S.A.

item.title: Global mutual insurance sector reaches record $1.61 trillion in premiums: ICMIF
item.description: According to the latest Global Mutual Market Share (MMS) report published today by the International Cooperative and Mutual Insurance Federation (ICMIF), mutual and cooperative insurers generated record premium income in 2024 of $1.61 trillion, while maintaining a 26.1% share of the global insurance market. The Global Mutual Market Share 2026 report explained that the 2024 reported data sits in comparison to 2023’s $1.50 trillion, with a 26% market share, which has broadly remained unchanged, demonstrating the continued resilience of the mutual insurance model. There are approximately 856 million members and policyholders currently being served globally, and around 1.2 million people employed across the global mutual insurance sector.

item.title: Ascot names James Lee as President of Leadline Capital Partners
item.description: Global specialty re/insurance company Ascot Group Limited has appointed James Lee as President of Leadline Capital Partners, Ascot’s dedicated third-party capital management platform launched in August 2024 . Building on Leadline Capital Partners’ strong foundation and growth trajectory, Lee will reportedly oversee the strategic direction and market development of the firm, while also leading its ILS initiatives and global capital raising and investor engagement efforts. He will be based in the company’s Stamford, CT office, reporting to Marc Amen, CEO, North America, who commented, “James is a proven leader with deep experience across insurance capital markets and insurance-linked securities.

item.title: Florida Teen Suing Meta Over Mental Health Harm Drops Claims Days Before Trial
item.description: A Florida teen dropped his lawsuit on Wednesday against Meta Platforms META.O , canceling a Los Angeles trial over claims the social media company’s apps caused his depression and anxiety. The trial, which had been slated to begin on Monday, was expected to be the second trial in thousands of lawsuits that have been consolidated in California state court over claims social media companies designed their platforms to be addictive to children, spurring a youth mental health crisis. “In light of the overall successful result of the litigation and his concerns about enduring a grueling weeks-long trial, he has elected to withdraw his claims against Meta,” attorneys for R.K.C.

item.title: Can Captive’s Hands-on Vetting of Bars Provide Answer to SC Liquor Liability Crisis?
item.description: He believes the captive approach, authorized by 2025 state legislation , can fill the gap and provide affordable liability insurance by doing something most insurance carriers don’t—intense, hands-on vetting of establishments, partly through surprise inspections and secret-shopper visits to prospective clients. For almost eight years now, South Carolina bars, restaurants and music venues have faced a sobering situation brought on by a 2018 state law that requires at least $1 million in liquor liability coverage: Pay unaffordable premiums or shut their doors altogether. An investment manager-turned-insurance executive and a group of like-minded people in the hospitality industry believe they’ve finally come up with at least a partial solution: A captive insurance company that can provide coverage at one-third to one-half of the price that traditional insurance companies have offered for the state-mandated coverage.

item.title: Cytora Adds InformData People Data
item.description: (Image source: InformData website.) Cytora (London) has integrated InformData (Kennesaw, Ga.) people data into its digital risk processing platform for commercial insurers. The integration gives insurers using Cytora access to InformData’s verifiable people data within workflows for submissions, renewals, claims and mid-term adjustments, according to a joint statement from the companies. “InformData provides a powerful way for commercial insurers to have a complete view of risk, extending beyond property and peril data to encompass the foundational element of people data,” comments Juan de Castro, COO, Cytora.

item.title: New LMA wording voids cover vessels making Hormuz toll payment
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allianz strikes US$2.1bn deal for HSBC Life Singapore

item.title: Gargantuan data centres present one of the greatest opportunities for premium income in a generation
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Mapfre to acquire Safety Insurance for $1.54 billion
item.description: Mapfre has agreed to acquire Safety Insurance Group in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.54 billion. Following the transaction, it will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Mapfre USA and continue operating under the Safety brand. The deal was unanimously approved by Safety’s board and approved by Mapfre’s board.

item.title: Root Launches Auto Insurance in New Jersey
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Root (Columbus, Ohio) has launched its behavior-based auto insurance in New Jersey, expanding the company’s availability to 37 states. The expansion brings Root’s mobile-first auto insurance offering to more than 6.6 million eligible drivers in New Jersey, according to a company statement. Root uses mobile telematics to price auto insurance based primarily on driving behavior, including focused driving, smooth braking and gentle turns.

item.title: Judge Casts Doubt on 69,000 Cases Alleging J&J Talc Caused Cancer
item.description: A federal judge cast doubt on Wednesday on claims by approximately 69,000 people alleging that Johnson & Johnson’s baby powder and other talc products caused ovarian cancer, saying plaintiffs must provide more specific evidence or risk having their lawsuits dismissed. Magistrate Judge Rukhsanah Singh in Trenton, New Jersey, said that recent testimony by two of the plaintiffs’ experts raised doubts about whether any plaintiffs can provide evidence admissible in court “that talcum powder use specifically caused her ovarian cancer.” Related: Jury Finds Johnson & Johnson Liable for Cancer in Latest Talc Trial The two experts, Judith Wolf and Daniel Clarke-Pearson, testified in May in preparation for a set of six “bellwether,” or test, trials for the thousands of cases that have been consolidated in New Jersey’s federal court. J&J has denied the allegations that its talc products caused cancer, saying that talc was safe and did not contain asbestos.

item.title: BMW Recalling 318,000 US Vehicles Over Starter Motor Overheating Risk
item.description: Related: Ford Recalling 288,000 US Vehicles Over Loose Roof Rails The recall affects certain models including Toyota Supra, 4 Series Convertible, 4 Series Coupe, 3 Series, X4, and X3 vehicles. vehicles as an engine starter may overheat and catch fire, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Thursday. As part of the remedy, dealers will replace the engine starter, free of charge.

item.title: Florida’s Share of US Claims Lawsuits Down to Nearly Half of 2020 Numbers, Data Show
item.description: By the end of 2025, three years after legislative reforms were adopted, that ratio was still a little disproportionate but it has ameliorated significantly: Florida homeowners accounted for about 5% of claims filed in the country, but the share of lawsuits has dropped to just 41%, the OIR noted in its latest Property Insurance Stability Report. in 2020, but more than 79% of the HO claims lawsuits. At the height of the Florida property insurance crisis in the early 2020s, one statistic seemed to galvanize the movement for reforming the state’s insurance claims litigation statutes: Floridians had produced about 9% of all homeowners’ claims in the U.S.

item.title: Overstored Pool Chemicals, Corroded Sprinklers Led to Georgia Chemical Plant Fire: Report
item.description: More than twice the amount of swimming pool chemicals as planned were improperly stored at a Georgia plant, setting conditions for a fire in 2024 that sent a huge plume of orange and black smoke into the sky and forced residents to shelter in place, a federal agency report concluded. That was shortly after the company began storing the chemicals at the plant, the agency said in its final report on the fire Tuesday. Chemical Safety and Hazard Investigation Board found that components of a sprinkler system inside the BioLab plant “had been experiencing significant corrosion and leaking” since 2019.

item.title: AI ‘Kill Switch’ Bill Floated by U.S. House Lawmakers
item.description: The legislation, called the “AI Kill Switch Act,” is backed by Democrat Ted Lieu and Republican Nathaniel Moran. Department of Homeland Security to intervene in what the bill calls a “loss-of-control scenario,” defined as the AI model carrying out a risky action that was not intended by the developer, according to the text of the bill. The lawmakers announced the legislation days after OpenAI said its AI agent went rogue during a security test and triggered a hack that compromised the infrastructure of AI startup Hugging Face .

item.title: Arizona Highlights 24% Reduction in Vehicle Thefts YoY
item.description: Despite a vehicle being stolen every 41 minutes in the state, the Arizona Automobile Theft Authority (AATA) reported a 24% decrease in year-to-date vehicle thefts compared with the first half of 2025. Last year, five of the first six months saw vehicle thefts exceeding 1,100 per month, for a total of 6,959 thefts. Vehicle thefts around the Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport area (85034) have increased significantly, with a 65% spike in the first six months compared to last year.

item.title: John McLean rejoins QBE as senior claims officer
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: RenRe’s property cat book still rate adequate, tactics evolve with the market: CEO O’Donnell
item.description: RenaissanceRe’s property catastrophe reinsurance portfolio remains rate adequate at today’s pricing, CEO Kevin O’Donnell said today, laying out the shift in tactics the reinsurer employed in response to market conditions at the mid-year renewals. We’ve been navigating the property cat market for decades and know when to grow and when to exercise discipline.” O’Donnell went on to say that the growth RenaissanceRe has experienced through the hard market, as well as through its acquisition of the Validus business, has positioned the company well for the current market environment. Speaking during the RenaissanceRe earnings call today, CEO Kevin O’Donnell provided some clarity on the way his company has positioned itself and its portfolios to continue extracting the best possible returns from the property catastrophe reinsurance business it underwrites.

item.title: Ascot Group hires James Lee as President of Leadline Capital Partners
item.description: Ascot Group, the specialist global re/insurance underwriter, has hired former Aspen Capital Markets executive James Lee as President of its dedicated third-party reinsurance capital management division Leadline Capital Partners. As President of Leadline Capital Partners, Lee will be tasked with overseeing the strategic direction and market development of its third-party capital strategies, while also leading on Ascot’s insurance-linked securities (ILS) initiatives, global capital raising and investor engagement work. Leadline Capital Partners was launched by Ascot in 2024 .

item.title: Brown & Brown selects Anthropic, McKinsey and Accenture to support AI transformation
item.description: To help execute this, Brown & Brown has enlisted Anthropic, McKinsey & Company and Accenture as partners, combining expertise in frontier AI, business transformation and governance to establish the guardrails, operating discipline and execution model required to scale AI responsibly across the enterprise. has announced that it is establishing artificial intelligence (AI) as a foundational enterprise capability, selecting Anthropic, McKinsey & Company and Accenture as partners to help rewire the business and scale AI responsibly across the organisation. By combining Anthropic’s advanced AI capabilities with Accenture’s experience designing technology architectures, developing implementation roadmaps and supporting business transformation, this collaboration is focused on accelerating innovation, modernising how work gets done and turning AI investments into measurable business outcomes.”

item.title: AXA XL appoints Grace Xu as Senior Underwriter, Captives in Los Angeles
item.description: AXA XL, the property, casualty and specialty risk division of AXA, has appointed Grace Xu as Senior Underwriter, Captives, within its Alternative Risk Solutions team. Xu joins AXA XL following more than three years at Starr Insurance, where she most recently served as Underwriter, Captive Reinsurance Fronting in Los Angeles. Based in Los Angeles, California, she has taken up the role as the business continues to strengthen its captive insurance and alternative risk capabilities.

item.title: RenRe CEO says firm is well positioned for softer market after decades of sound cycle management
item.description: O’Donnell said the company has spent decades navigating the property catastrophe market and has developed a clear understanding of when to expand and when to remain disciplined. He added that property catastrophe rates remain broadly adequate and continue to guide the firm’s underwriting approach. During the earnings call, O’Donnell said the performance reflected the long-term, disciplined execution of RenaissanceRe’s strategy, which has positioned the company to continue expanding.

item.title: Florida Appeals Court Strikes Jury’s $335,000 Award in Universal Plumbing Claim
item.description: One of Florida’s largest property insurers won a major victory Wednesday from an appeals court, which found that a jury’s $335,000 award in a claims dispute over plumbing damages was far out of line with the evidence. “The verdict was several times higher than the only admitted valuation evidence, so it does not bear a reasonable relation to the damages proved,” Florida’s 1st District Court of Appeals wrote in Karmo vs. “Somehow, the jury returned a verdict awarding plaintiff $335,000 in RCV damages and $305,000 in ACV damages,” Universal’s attorney, Paulo Lima, wrote in his appeals brief.

item.title: War Risk Insurance Costs Surge for Southern Red Sea Voyages After Houthi Attacks
item.description: On Thursday, the sources said, indicative war risk premiums for voyages through the southern Red Sea rose to over 1% of the value of a ship from around ⁠0.75% on Tuesday and 0.3% last week before the Houthi announcement, according to the sources, who declined to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter. The insurance cost of shipping goods through the southern Red Sea doubled for some companies on Thursday, after Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthis attacked at least one tanker overnight, sources said. Rates for some shipping companies calling at Saudi ports and also Saudi-linked ships were quoted as high as 3% for voyages from southern Saudi ports in the Red Sea such as Jizan and Al Shuqaiq, which would take them closer to Yemeni territory and for transits through the Bab el-Mandeb gateway further south that leads into the Gulf of Aden, the sources added.

item.title: People Moves: Brown & Riding Names Kane to National Casualty Practice
item.description: Brown & Riding to announced that Abe Kane has joined its National Casualty Practice. With over 20 years of industry experience, Kane specializes in casualty solutions for public entities and higher education institutions. Kane has extensive experience structuring complex insurance programs across a broad spectrum of coverages, including property, equipment breakdown, general liability, commercial auto liability and physical damage, excess and umbrella liability, workers’ compensation, and management and professional liability coverages, such as public officials’ liability, law enforcement liability, educators legal liability, and employment practices liability.

item.title: It Wasn't a Decision. It Was a Default.
item.description: The room did what rooms do under time pressure: it ran with the fastest, most defensible read, and by the time anyone might have asked a second question, the decision was already operational. By default, the answer is savings, because savings is the fastest, most comfortable story a room on the clock can agree on. Part of why this default never gets challenged is that the dashboard isn't built to challenge it.

item.title: Coverwatch raises $4.5 million
item.description: Insurance broker Coverwatch has raised a $4.5 million pre-seed round led by CoFound and Restive, with participation from KFund, liquid2 ventures and others. Founded in 2025 and based in San Francisco, Coverwatch focuses on complex commercial risk. Coverwatch is currently licensed in 17 states and expects to be licensed in all 48 continental U.S.

item.title: Le Garde prepares handbag insurance offering
item.description: Le Garde, a platform for valuing luxury handbags, appears to be preparing an insurance product for high-value bags. The company’s website is also teasing a forthcoming insurance offering promising worldwide coverage, no deductible and protection equal to 150% of the insured value. The company filed a US trademark application for KLASPP on July 8, covering insurance brokerage and the appraisal and valuation of personal property, specifically bags.

item.title: Winnebago partners with Progressive
item.description: Winnebago Industries has partnered with Progressive to connect RV owners with the insurer’s coverage solutions. Our collaboration with Winnebago brings together two trusted brands to help simplify the RV ownership journey, offering customers a more connected experience from purchase through protection.” – Eric Doubler, Progressive recreational lines direct business leader. “At Progressive, we’re committed to making it easier for customers to protect what matters most and enjoy the road ahead with confidence.

item.title: Beyond the Flood Zone: Managing Urban Flooding in the Northeast
item.description: By expanding their understanding of flood risk in addition to using FEMA’s legacy maps, carriers can better position themselves for urban flooding. Flooding in northeastern urban environments involves decidedly murkier waters. A massive exposure gap exists in many of these northeastern urban areas because they largely fall outside traditional, FEMA-designated flood zones.

item.title: Secondary Perils Responsible for Virtually All 2025 Nat-Cat Insured Losses in North America: Swiss Re
item.description: hurricane landfall, these secondary perils made up 99.9% of 2025 insured natural catastrophe losses in the region. North America recorded more than $90 billion in insured natural catastrophe losses in 2025, down from $117 billion in 2024 and below the five-year average of $97 billion. Severe convective storms caused $46 billion in losses in North America, making 2025 the third-costliest year on record for that peril, Swiss Re said.

item.title: Asia insurance rates decline 5% in Q2, led by cyber: Marsh
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Indonesia’s Asei downgraded to BB+ by Fitch on capital strain, loss pressures
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Australia motor body calls out IAG’s ‘fig leaf’ remedy, urges ACCC to block RAC Insurance acquisition
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Managing conflicts of interest in insurance
item.description: When a consumer buys insurance, they need to trust that the firm they're dealing with is genuinely working toward the best outcome for them – and that they’re not losing out due to conflicts of interest. But they can also create conflicts of interest – particularly if they influence consumer journeys or potentially alter commercial incentives. We've taken enforcement action before against firms where conflicts of interest weren't properly managed, and where ownership or remuneration arrangements influenced customer outcomes.

item.title: Is On-Prem Making A Comeback?
item.description: Here are three trends that I believe are pushing on-prem back into the limelight. A PBX vendor recently told me something I did not expect to hear: customers are asking for on-premise systems again. Looking broader into the entire market, I can see how this makes a lot of sense.
